metadata.title:
G. MS BIG LANDSLIDE


is FINALLY OVERCOME

pages.raw_text:
Officials of the Great Northern
railroad today stated that work
men have just overcome what is
probably the greatest and most
dangerous landslide in the history
of the section, by building what In
railroad parlance is known as
"shoo-fly" around the big landslide
IL miles east of Boomers Ferry,
Idaho. In the case of most slides,
as soon as men can be rushed to
the scene, shovels and picks are
busy by the hundreds and the de-
lays to trains is only a few hours.
But in the case Of the great slide in
Idaho, the company instantly set
to work building a track around it.
In the meantime, passengers and
baggage would be transferred from
